entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A staple food crop (Pennisetum glaucum) cultivated by small farmers in Niger.

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The country where pearl millet is cultivated and the study on sheep feed is conducted.

entities.name: STOVER
entities.description: The residues of the pearl millet plant used as basal diet for livestock.

entities.name: SHEEP
entities.description: Young small ruminants used to test the effect of dual-purpose pearl millet residues on growth.

entities.name: COLD DRY SEASON
entities.description: The environmental condition during which stover is primarily used as livestock feed.

entities.name: DUAL-PURPOSE VARIETIES
entities.description: Pearl millet varieties developed by ICRISAT to provide both grain for food and stover for livestock.

entities.name: FEED QUALITY
entities.description: The nutritional value of the stover from dual-purpose pearl millet varieties.

entities.name: FEED INTAKE
entities.description: The amount of feed consumed by young sheep.

entities.name: LIVE WEIGHT CHANGES
entities.description: The variation in body weight of young sheep fed with pearl millet residues.

entities.name: GROWTH PERFORMANCE
entities.description: The overall growth and development metrics of sheep fed with pearl millet residues.

entities.name: EVALUATION
entities.description: The research process used to assess feed quality and growth performance.
